# Harbor Design System ‚Äî full documentation

> A token-driven design system. Components are documented from their Figma source; every color, size, and type value resolves to a design token.

---

# Token architecture

---

# Color

---

# Typography

---

# Dimensions

---

# Button

Buttons let people take an action, confirm a choice, or move forward in a flow.
They're the primary way of triggering something to happen on screen.

> _Live, interactive example ‚Äî see this component in Storybook._

## Anatomy

A button has two parts: a **container** (background, border, corner radius, focus
ring) and a **label** (one line of centered text). There's no icon slot and no size
property yet ‚Äî every button renders at the same padding scale (24px horizontal,
12px vertical, 8px corner radius).

## Types

`variant` sets visual weight, not just color.

- **Primary** ‚Äî highest emphasis. Reserve for the one action that matters most in a
  given context: submitting a form, confirming a purchase, completing a step. Solid fill.
- **Secondary** ‚Äî medium emphasis. For actions that matter but shouldn't outrank the
  primary one, like "Add another" next to "Save."
- **Outline** ‚Äî lowest emphasis. For optional or reversible actions like "Cancel" or
  "Back," or when several actions sit side by side and none should dominate.

Rule of thumb: one Primary button per view. If two actions feel equally important, the
flow needs rethinking before the button hierarchy does.

## States

All three types share the same six states: Enabled, Hover, Pressed, Focus, Disabled, Loading.

- **Hover / Pressed** darken the fill (or border, for Outline) to confirm interactivity.
  Outline gives Hover and Pressed two distinct border shades, so the states read differently
  instead of looking identical.
- **Focus** adds a two-layer ring ‚Äî a white gap plus a colored outer ring ‚Äî shared across all
  three types via one token pair, so focus looks the same regardless of button type.
- **Disabled** flattens background and text to neutral gray. Outline keeps its white
  background and only dims border and label; the dimmed label color matches how Disabled
  reads on Primary and Secondary.
- **Loading** keeps the type's fill and marks the button busy. There is no spinner ‚Äî pass a
  "Loading‚Ä¶" label yourself; the component only handles the state, not the text.

In code, Hover/Pressed/Focus are driven by `:hover` / `:active` / `:focus-visible`,
Disabled by the native `disabled` attribute, and Loading by the `loading` prop
(which sets `aria-busy` and blocks interaction).

## Color reference

Resolved value **and the token assigned to it** for every type and state. Values come
from the built component tokens; the state‚Üítoken mapping is what `Button.css` actually
references. Token names below drop the per-variant prefix shown above each table.

**Focus** has no fill token of its own ‚Äî it keeps the Enabled background/content and
adds the shared ring (`--ds-semantic-focus-ring-*` / `--ds-semantic-focus-gap-*`).

**Primary** ‚Äî prefix `--ds-component-button-primary-color-`. Content is `#FFFFFF` in every state.

| State    | Background                       | Content                       |
|----------|----------------------------------|-------------------------------|
| Enabled  | `#9B7FFF` ¬∑ `background-enabled`  | `#FFFFFF` ¬∑ `content-enabled`  |
| Hover    | `#834DFF` ¬∑ `background-hover`    | `#FFFFFF` ¬∑ `content-hover`    |
| Pressed  | `#6C31DB` ¬∑ `background-pressed`  | `#FFFFFF` ¬∑ `content-pressed`  |
| Focus    | `#9B7FFF` ¬∑ `background-enabled`  | `#FFFFFF` ¬∑ `content-enabled`  |
| Disabled | `#CFD5DA` ¬∑ `background-disabled` | `#FFFFFF` ¬∑ `content-disabled` |
| Loading  | `#9B7FFF` ¬∑ `background-loading`  | `#FFFFFF` ¬∑ `content-loading`  |

**Secondary** ‚Äî prefix `--ds-component-button-secondary-color-`. Content is `#FFFFFF` in every state.

| State    | Background                       | Content                       |
|----------|----------------------------------|-------------------------------|
| Enabled  | `#F36700` ¬∑ `background-enabled`  | `#FFFFFF` ¬∑ `content-enabled`  |
| Hover    | `#C75300` ¬∑ `background-hover`    | `#FFFFFF` ¬∑ `content-hover`    |
| Pressed  | `#A14200` ¬∑ `background-pressed`  | `#FFFFFF` ¬∑ `content-pressed`  |
| Focus    | `#F36700` ¬∑ `background-enabled`  | `#FFFFFF` ¬∑ `content-enabled`  |
| Disabled | `#CFD5DA` ¬∑ `background-disabled` | `#FFFFFF` ¬∑ `content-disabled` |
| Loading  | `#F36700` ¬∑ `background-loading`  | `#FFFFFF` ¬∑ `content-loading`  |

**Outline** ‚Äî prefix `--ds-component-button-outline-color-`. White background, visible border in every state.

| State    | Background                        | Content                       | Border                     |
|----------|-----------------------------------|-------------------------------|----------------------------|
| Enabled  | `#FFFFFF` ¬∑ `background-enabled`   | `#484E53` ¬∑ `content-enabled`  | `#B0B9BF` ¬∑ `border-enabled`  |
| Hover    | `#F2F4F5` ¬∑ `background-hover`     | `#1B1D1F` ¬∑ `content-hover`    | `#909AA1` ¬∑ `border-hover`    |
| Pressed  | `#E3E7E9` ¬∑ `background-pressed`   | `#1B1D1F` ¬∑ `content-pressed`  | `#747D84` ¬∑ `border-pressed`  |
| Focus    | `#FFFFFF` ¬∑ `background-enabled`   | `#484E53` ¬∑ `content-enabled`  | `#B0B9BF` ¬∑ `border-enabled`  |
| Disabled | `#FFFFFF` ¬∑ `background-enabled` ‚ÄÝ | `#CFD5DA` ¬∑ `content-disabled` | `#CFD5DA` ¬∑ `border-disabled` |
| Loading  | `#FFFFFF` ¬∑ `background-loading`   | `#484E53` ¬∑ `content-loading`  | `#B0B9BF` ¬∑ `border-loading`  |

‚ÄÝ Outline keeps its **enabled** (white) background when disabled ‚Äî only content and
border drop to their disabled tokens. The `‚Ä¶-background-disabled` token (`#CFD5DA`) exists
but is intentionally not used here.

## Specs

| Property        | Value | Token |
|-----------------|-------|-------|
| Padding (horizontal) | 24px | `--ds-component-button-padding-horizontal` |
| Padding (vertical)   | 12px | `--ds-component-button-padding-vertical` |
| Corner radius        | 8px  | `--ds-component-button-border-radius` |
| Border width         | 1px  | `--ds-component-button-border-width` |

**Typography** ‚Äî semantic tier `label/lg`.

| Font      | Weight    | Size | Line height | Letter spacing |
|-----------|-----------|------|-------------|----------------|
| Helvetica | Bold (700) | 16px | 24px        | 0              |

## Usage guidelines

- Choose type by hierarchy, not by which one looks better in the moment.
- Outline needs a visible border in every state, including Disabled ‚Äî don't let it disappear
  just because the background is white.
- Keep labels short and verb-led ("Save changes," not "Changes will be saved").

## Examples

```tsx
// Primary action
<Button variant="primary" onClick={handleSubmit}>Save changes</Button>

// Secondary and outline side by side
<Button variant="secondary">Add another</Button>
<Button variant="outline">Cancel</Button>

// Loading state ‚Äî pass the busy label yourself
<Button variant="primary" loading>Saving‚Ä¶</Button>
```

## Component API

```tsx
```

| Prop       | Type                                        | Default     | Description |
|------------|---------------------------------------------|-------------|-------------|
| `variant`  | `'primary' \| 'secondary' \| 'outline'`     | `'primary'` | Visual style. Maps to the Figma `Type` axis. |
| `loading`  | `boolean`                                   | `false`     | Applies the loading colors, sets `aria-busy`, and blocks interaction. |
| `disabled` | `boolean`                                   | `false`     | Native disabled attribute. Removes the button from interaction and the tab order. |
| `children` | `React.ReactNode`                           | ‚Äî           | Button label. One line of centered text. **Required.** |
| `...rest`  | `React.ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement>` | ‚Äî       | All native button attributes (`onClick`, `type`, `aria-*`, ‚Ä¶) forward to the underlying `<button>`. |

## Accessibility

- The focus ring is mandatory and shared across types ‚Äî restyle it, don't suppress it.
- Disabled buttons stay in the layout so people know the action exists, just not right now.
- Loading is announced to assistive tech: the component sets `aria-busy="true"`, not only a
  visual change.

## Tokens used

Every value resolves to a design token built from the Figma `Button` component set ‚Äî no
hardcoded colors or sizes.

**Color ‚Äî component tier**

- `--ds-component-button-primary-color-background-{enabled, hover, pressed, disabled, loading}`
- `--ds-component-button-primary-color-content-{enabled, hover, pressed, disabled, loading}`
- `--ds-component-button-secondary-color-background-{enabled, hover, pressed, disabled, loading}`
- `--ds-component-button-secondary-color-content-{enabled, hover, pressed, disabled, loading}`
- `--ds-component-button-outline-color-background-{enabled, hover, pressed, loading}` ‚Äî focus and disabled resolve through `enabled`
- `--ds-component-button-outline-color-content-{enabled, hover, pressed, disabled, loading}`
- `--ds-component-button-outline-color-border-{enabled, hover, pressed, disabled, loading}`

**Spacing & shape ‚Äî component tier**

- `--ds-component-button-padding-horizontal` (24px)
- `--ds-component-button-padding-vertical` (12px)
- `--ds-component-button-border-radius` (8px)
- `--ds-component-button-border-width` (1px)

**Typography ‚Äî semantic tier (`label/lg`)**

- `--ds-semantic-typography-label-lg-{font-family, font-size, line-height, letter-spacing, font-weight}` ‚Äî Helvetica Bold, 16/24px

**Focus ‚Äî semantic tier**

- `--ds-semantic-focus-ring-{color, x, y, blur, spread}`
- `--ds-semantic-focus-gap-{color, x, y, blur, spread}`
